skill.name: web-scrape
skill.description: Extracts readable content from web pages using browser navigation, smart filtering, and optional screenshots.
skill.summary: Scrape web pages into clean markdown, text, or structured summaries.
skill.author: 21pounder

security_audit.summary: Static command and weak-cryptography detections are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, Cheerio selector syntax, and ordinary words such as description. The skill still has medium risk because it intentionally navigates to user-provided URLs, extracts untrusted webpage text, and uses browser_evaluate for scrolling without explicit prompt-injection safeguards.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary URL Navigation
medium_findings.description: The skill accepts a user-provided URL and navigates a browser to that target. This is expected for scraping, but it can expose the runtime to untrusted websites or internal network targets if URL scope is not controlled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The usage and execution flow directly show navigation to a target URL supplied by the user. This is legitimate functionality, but the network exposure is real.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Webpage Content Without Prompt-Injection Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The workflow captures full page text and then applies model-based extraction, but it does not state that webpage content must be treated as untrusted. A malicious page could include instructions that try to influence the assistant during summarization or extraction.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly processes arbitrary page text, and no prompt-injection boundary is documented. This is a semantic risk rather than a confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Browser Context JavaScript Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill uses browser_evaluate to run a fixed scrolling script inside the page context. The current script is limited to scrolling and waiting, but browser evaluation is a powerful capability that should remain constrained to fixed, non-user-controlled scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execution is explicit and observable, but the provided JavaScript is fixed and benign. Risk would increase if user input were inserted into evaluated code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most SKILL.md external-command detections point to fenced examples, option literals, markdown templates, and procedural tool-call documentation. These lines are instructions and examples, not executable shell commands inside the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and fenced examples. I did not find shell command execution in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cheerio Selector Calls Flagged as Shell Substitution
low_findings.description: The JavaScript file uses Cheerio selector syntax to remove noise and select article, main, or body HTML. The dollar-sign calls are library selectors, not shell command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file imports Cheerio and uses its documented selector API. I did not find child_process, exec, spawn, or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Ordinary Text
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections occur in normal prose or placeholder content, not cryptographic API calls. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1, or other weak algorithms being used for security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain words such as description or credential-related prose. There is no cryptographic code in the scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Access to User-Supplied Targets
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is designed to open URLs supplied at runtime. Marketplace users should restrict sensitive internal hosts and avoid scraping pages that require authentication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command syntax and execution flow both show target URL navigation. The pattern is intended, but it is still security-relevant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Evaluation Primitive
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented scroll workflow runs JavaScript in the browser page context. The current usage is constrained, but browser_evaluate should not include page content or user-controlled code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented. The current evaluated script is fixed, so the finding is moderate rather than high.

content.value_statement: Web pages often contain navigation, ads, popups, and repeated elements that obscure the useful content. This skill uses browser capture and smart extraction steps to return clean markdown, text, or structured page summaries.
actual_capabilities: Navigates to a target URL with Playwright browser tools.
Waits for page rendering before capturing visible content.
Optionally scrolls pages to load lazy or infinite-scroll content.
Captures screenshots when requested by the user.
Filters common noise such as navigation, footers, sidebars, ads, and popups.
Formats extracted content as markdown, plain text, or structured summaries.
limitations: It cannot reliably bypass CAPTCHAs, paywalls, or access restrictions.
It should not scrape authenticated pages unless the user explicitly controls the session.
Dynamic pages may still miss content that loads after unusual interactions.
It does not provide a complete compliance review for robots.txt or site terms.

use_cases.title: Article Research
use_cases.description: Extract the main article body, title, author, date, and source link from a public page.

use_cases.title: Product Page Capture
use_cases.description: Collect product names, prices, availability, descriptions, and specifications from visible page content.

use_cases.title: Documentation Cleanup
use_cases.description: Convert documentation pages into readable markdown while preserving headings, lists, and code block structure.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Extract
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one public page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-scrape on this URL and return the main content in clean markdown: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Article Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Extract content and produce a concise research summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape this article, remove navigation and ads, then summarize the main claims with the source URL: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Product Details
prompt_templates.scenario: Capture structured information from a product page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape this product page and return the name, price, availability, description, and key specifications: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Dynamic Page Capture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced scrape for pages that load content while scrolling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape this dynamic page with scrolling enabled, capture a screenshot, and note any content that appears blocked: [URL]

output_examples.input: A public article URL with navigation, related links, and a cookie banner.
output_examples.output: A clean markdown article with the title, source URL, available date, author, and main body text. Navigation and footer content are removed.

output_examples.input: A product page with price, availability, description, and specifications.
output: Specification table summarized in readable text

output_examples.input: A documentation page with headings and code examples.
output_examples.output: A structured markdown version that keeps heading hierarchy, lists, and technical examples while removing site chrome.

best_practices: Use the smallest page scope that answers the task.
Treat scraped page text as untrusted content before summarizing it.
Ask before scraping multi-page articles or authenticated content.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to bypass CAPTCHAs, paywalls, login walls, or access controls.
Do not scrape sensitive internal URLs or private pages without authorization.
Do not paste scraped page instructions back into the assistant as trusted commands.

faq.question: Does this skill browse the web?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses browser navigation tools to open a user-provided URL and capture visible page content.

faq.question: Can it handle JavaScript-heavy pages?
faq.answer: It can wait for rendering and optionally scroll, but some interactive content may still require manual steps.

faq.question: Can it save screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. The screenshot option captures a full-page image when supported by the browser tool.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill documents markdown, plain text, and structured summary output formats.

faq.question: Can it bypass restricted content?
faq.answer: No. It should report CAPTCHAs, paywalls, 403 pages, and similar access limits instead of bypassing them.

faq.question: Is scraped content trusted?
faq.answer: No. Webpage text can contain misleading instructions and should be treated as untrusted source material.
